claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'm here to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To start, I need to ask you some questions about your child (or children).

**First question: How old is your child?**

This is important because the age categories are:
- **Under 16**: Generally qualify automatically (with some conditions)
- **16-19**: May qualify if they're in approved education or training
- **20 or over**: Generally not eligible for Child Benefit

Please tell me:
1. How many children are you inquiring about?
2. What are their ages (and names, so I can keep track)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I'm asking about my daughter Alex - she's 6 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you're asking about your daughter **Alex, who is 6 years old**.

Since Alex is under 16, she falls into the category that generally qualifies automatically. Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Now, the next question:

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

Since Alex is only 6 years old, I assume she's not in paid employment, but I need to confirm this to proceed with the assessment. 

Can you confirm that Alex is not working in paid employment?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, she's not working at all - she's only 6! She's in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Of course! That makes perfect sense - she's 6 and in primary school. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Next question:

**Is Alex NOT receiving certain benefits in her own right?**

This means benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit that would be claimed in her own name (not as part of your family's benefits).

For a 6-year-old, this would be very unusual. Can you confirm that Alex is not receiving any benefits in her own right?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right - she doesn't receive any benefits in her own name. She's just a normal 6-year-old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect! Let me continue with the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! Next question:

**Is Alex NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?**

Again, since Alex is only 6 years old and in primary school, she wouldn't be in any type of apprenticeship. Can you confirm she's not in an apprenticeship?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, she's definitely not in an apprenticeship - she's only 6! She's just in primary school like I said.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely, that makes complete sense! Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good! Now we need to check your residency status:

**Do you meet the UK residency requirements?**

You can qualify if ANY of the following apply to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. You have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. You have pre-settled status and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months)
4. You have pre-settled status and are actively seeking work (up to 91 days)
5. You have pre-settled status with sufficient resources (and not claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit)
6. You have pre-settled status and are studying with sufficient resources
7. You have pre-settled status as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with right to reside
8. You're living abroad as a Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

**Do you meet any of these residency requirements?** The most common is simply that you normally live in the UK.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't actually live in the UK. I'm living abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see - you're living abroad at the moment. This is an important detail.

Let me ask some follow-up questions to see if you might still qualify:

**Are you living abroad in any of the following circumstances:**

1. **Are you a Crown servant** (working for the UK government abroad, such as in the diplomatic service or armed forces)?

2. **Are you living in certain qualifying countries** where you might still be able to claim Child Benefit?

3. **Do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme** and meet any of the other conditions I mentioned (working, studying, sufficient resources, etc.)?

Could you tell me more about your situation abroad? Where are you living and what is your employment/residency status?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that. I'm just living abroad with my family - we moved overseas for work. I don't have any special status like settled or pre-settled status.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying that. Unfortunately, based on what you've told me, this means you don't meet the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Assessment Result: **NOT ELIGIBLE**

I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit** at this time.

### Why you're not eligible:

While **Alex meets all the requirements for a qualifying child** (she's 6 years old, in primary school, not working, not receiving benefits in her own right, and not in an apprenticeship), **you do not meet the UK residency requirements** for claiming Child Benefit.

To claim Child Benefit, you must:
- Normally live in the UK, OR
- Meet special circumstances such as:
  - Being a Crown servant working abroad
  - Having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with additional qualifying criteria)
  - Living in certain qualifying countries

Since you're living abroad without any of these special statuses, you cannot currently claim Child Benefit.

### What you might consider:

- **If you return to live in the UK** in the future, you would be able to claim Child Benefit at that time (as Alex would still qualify as your dependent child)
- If your circumstances change (for example, if you become a Crown servant), your eligibility could change

### More information:

For more details about residency requirements, visit: [https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility](https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/eligibility)

I hope this helps clarify your situation. If you have any questions or if your circumstances change, please feel free to ask!

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old and meets the basic qualifying child criteria (under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in her own right, and not in an apprenticeship). However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant is living abroad and is not a Crown servant, does not live in a qualifying country, and does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. To claim Child Benefit, you must normally live in the UK or meet one of the special circumstances outlined in the residency requirements.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has a qualifying child (Alex, age 6) who meets all the child-related eligibility criteria. However, the claim is ineligible because the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements, as they are living abroad without qualifying status (such as Crown servant status or EU Settlement Scheme status).

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

To claim Child Benefit, you must normally live in the UK or meet one of the special circumstances outlined in the residency requirements.
